description: Battle log chronological ordering: add battle start/end markers, ensure logs appear in order, and fix incomplete log when war ends
steps: 1. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts resolveNextBattle function (around line 2000), add a log entry at the START of battle resolution: add '--- Inicio Batalla {N} ({provinceName}) ---' to newState.log right after battle.logStartIndex is set. The battle number N is unresolvedIdx + 1.
2. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts at the END of resolveNextBattle (around line 2418, after 'gana la batalla'), add '--- Batalla {N} resuelta ---' log entry. N is unresolvedIdx + 1.

3. For uncontested battles that are resolved in doAcceptBattlePopup (in gameStore.ts), also add start/end log markers when marking them as resolved. Add '--- Inicio Batalla {N} ({provinceName}) ---' and '--- Batalla {N} resuelta ---' entries.

4. Verify the log ordering: bonuses appear first (from applyWarUpgrades which runs before battles), then battle 1 start, battle 1 events, battle 1 resolved, battle 2 start, etc. The battle ordering should follow the activeBattles array order.

5. Investigate why the gamelog appears incomplete when war ends and the sidebar summary shows - this may be because the log entries from the last battle resolution are added AFTER the result popup is shown. Make sure the log is fully written before the war-complete summary appears on the sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: Each battle has '--- Inicio Batalla N (ProvinceName) ---' before its events and '--- Batalla N resuelta ---' after
Battles are logged chronologically (battle 1 fully before battle 2)
Uncontested battles also get start/end markers
Game log is complete when war ends and sidebar shows war results
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/shoguns-ascent && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
findings: Implementation added battle start/end markers in three locations: (1) resolveNextBattle start marker after logStartIndex is set, (2) resolveNextBattle end marker before battle.resolved = true, (3) doAcceptBattlePopup uncontested battles get both markers plus a descriptive message. The GameLog component already reads from gameState.log which contains all entries by the time the war summary shows, so no changes were needed there. tsc --noEmit and npm run build both pass.
